name: Oracle Hospitality OPERA Cloud REST API
what_it_does: Oracle Hospitality OPERA Cloud REST API for hotels, resorts, casinos, and hospitality enterprises to manage property operations including reservations, check-in/check-out, room management, revenue management, housekeeping, and guest profiles — enabling automated hotel operations orchestration, reservation management, revenue optimization, and guest experience automation through Oracle's industry-leading property management system used by major hotel chains worldwide. Enables AI agents to manage reservations for room booking and availability automation, handle check-in and check-out for guest arrival and departure automation, access room management for housekeeping and room status automation, retrieve rate management for pricing and revenue strategy automation, manage guest profiles for loyalty and preference management automation, handle folio management for billing and charge posting automation, access group reservations for block management and cut-off date automation, retrieve housekeeping management for room cleaning task automation, manage event management for meeting room and catering automation, and integrate OPERA with OTA channels, payment, and loyalty systems for end-to-end hotel operations automation.
use_cases: Automating hotel reservation management and room availability for hospitality automation agents
Managing check-in and check-out workflows for guest service automation agents
Optimizing room rates and availability for revenue management automation agents
Coordinating housekeeping assignments and room status for hotel operations automation agents
not_for: Short-term rental properties (OPERA Cloud is for hotels/resorts, not Airbnb-style STR management)
Restaurant-only operations without hotel (OPERA is primarily PMS-centric, though has F&B modules)
Consumer-facing booking (OPERA Cloud is backend PMS, not front-end booking engine for guests)
best_when: A hotel, resort, casino, or hospitality enterprise wanting AI agents to automate reservation management, guest check-in/out, room operations, revenue management, and housekeeping coordination through Oracle Hospitality's OPERA Cloud PMS platform.
avoid_when: ORACLE HOSPITALITY AGREEMENT REQUIRED: OPERA Cloud requires enterprise hotel agreement with Oracle Hospitality; automated developer-access assumption creates enterprise_contract_required; automated must be licensed OPERA Cloud customer. PROPERTY CONFIGURATION IS COMPLEX: OPERA Cloud properties require extensive configuration (room types, rate plans, packages); automated out-of-box assumption creates missing_configuration for properties without proper setup; automated must have properly configured OPERA Cloud property. INTEGRATIONS REQUIRE OPERA CONNECT: Third-party integrations use Oracle Hospitality Integration Platform (OHIP); automated direct-API assumption creates middleware_required for integrations not using OHIP; automated must use OHIP for certified integration partner access. OTA CHANNEL MANAGEMENT IS EXTERNAL: OTA rate and inventory management uses channel manager or OPERA Connector; automated built-in-OTA assumption creates distribution_gap without configured channel connectivity; automated must configure OTA channel manager separately.

auth.notes: Oracle Hospitality OPERA Cloud uses OAuth2 for PMS REST API. REST API with JSON. Austin, TX HQ (Oracle). Products: OPERA Cloud PMS, OPERA Cloud Sales & Events, Simphony (restaurant), MICROS POS, Nor1 (upselling). Used by Marriott, IHG, and 40,000+ hotels. Oracle Hospitality Integration Platform (OHIP) for partner integrations. Competes with Agilysys, Infor HMS, and Mews for enterprise hotel PMS.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Demo for qualified hotel enterprises; production requires Oracle Hospitality contract
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per property or room; OPERA Cloud Essentials, Standard, Premium tiers; SaaS consumption model
pricing.notes: Austin TX. Oracle subsidiary. Annual subscription per property. 40,000+ hotels. Industry-leading PMS.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; reservation and property APIs throttled per Oracle plan

known_agent_gotchas: OHIP AUTHENTICATION IS SEPARATE: Oracle Hospitality Integration Platform has its own authentication separate from OPERA Cloud; automated unified-auth assumption creates wrong_credential for OHIP vs direct OPERA API calls; automated must use correct authentication per integration path

CHAIN CODE IS REQUIRED: OPERA Cloud uses hotel chain code hierarchy; automated single-hotel assumption creates missing_chain_context for enterprise deployments with multiple properties under a chain; automated must include chain code in all property-level operations

RATE RESTRICTIONS ARE COMPLEX: Rate plans have restrictions (advance purchase, minimum stay, closed dates); automated open-rate assumption creates booking_error for rates with active restrictions; automated must check rate plan restrictions before booking

FOLIO CHARGES HAVE ROUTING RULES: Charge routing to different folios follows property-configured rules; automated single-folio assumption creates routing_error for charges that should route to master or separate folios; automated must respect charge routing configuration

HOUSEKEEPING STATUS HAS WORKFLOW: Room housekeeping status follows defined states (Dirty → Clean → Inspected); automated instant-clean assumption creates incorrect_status for rooms not yet inspected by housekeeping supervisor; automated must follow housekeeping workflow states
agent_readiness.security_notes: Hotel PMS. SOC2, PCI-DSS, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Guest, reservation, and payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Hospitality PMS. SOC2, PCI-DSS, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Hotel guest and payment data.
